title: ProtocolLab Secure DNS Contract Verification
scope: Verify secure DNS public contract structure only; do not query a resolver or verify an executor, server, implementation, or benchmark.
verification_method: Schema validation, deterministic DNS fixture inspection, suite and profile resolution, invalid-fixture checks, and standards-link inspection.
procedure: Validate every canonical DNS wire fixture, exact bare-message lengths and SHA-256 digests, and each declared length-prefixed encoding.
Confirm the bounded corpus contains A, AAAA, CNAME-chain, NXDOMAIN, NOERROR/NODATA, and large EDNS/DNSSEC-shaped semantics without a full transport cross-product.
Confirm each invalid DNS fixture fails its selected schema.

Validate both secure-DNS TLS profiles and confirm the exact v1 profile remains unchanged while the v2 profile permits only its declared version sets and requires recorded negotiated TLS, ALPN, cipher, group, signature, certificate, platform, and acceleration evidence.
Confirm each strict and interoperability scenario resolves endpoint identity, cryptographic policy, certificate policy, and transport ALPN from its selected profile.
Validate secure DNS and classic diagnostic scenarios and suite/profile resolution.
Confirm the four authoritative interoperability scenarios retain exact DoT, DoH2, DoH3, and DoQ bindings and do not permit transport fallback.
Confirm the resolver-role DoT and DoH2 fixtures set RD in the query, set RA and clear AA in the response, and retain exact wire lengths and hashes.

Confirm resolver-role scenarios require a selected recursive resolver, cache flush before every measured operation, no cache reuse, a runner-provided local fixture authority as the only upstream, no external upstream, and resolver plus upstream proof artifacts.
Confirm resolver-role scenarios are diagnostic and non-rankable, may be published as measured validation, and remain separate from authoritative-role cohorts.
Confirm DoH GET uses the exact unpadded base64url query parameter, has no request body, and retains response media type, cache, status, and normalized semantic validation.
Confirm DoQ declares one client-initiated bidirectional stream per transaction, client and server FIN, and RFC 9250 cancellation and error behavior.
Confirm DoT declares two-octet framing, pipelining, outstanding message-ID uniqueness, ID-plus-question response correlation, and no timed-out ID reuse before connection close.
Confirm classic UDP is a bare datagram, classic TCP uses two-octet framing, and TC on the 512-byte UDP calibration triggers one identical-question TCP retry for the full 630-byte response.
Confirm responses are semantic-validated and canonical-reserialized before normalized length and hash comparison.
Confirm connection, handshake, query, and drain timeouts plus zero retries are explicit.
Confirm required metrics, transport proof artifacts, diagnostic-only classic and resolver intent, publishable measured-validation status, and non-rankable comparison intent are explicit.
expected_result: The secure DNS family is deterministic, transport- and role-specific, and implementation-neutral; it exposes a portable standards baseline without weakening the strict diagnostic profile or implying executable support.
